messages.content: What is the maximum number of courses a student can take in one semester at Webb?
Six. The catalog says students are required to take five courses each semester, many take a sixth, and seven is not allowed. [source: course catalog]
Can a Webb student ever take seven classes in a semester?
No. The course catalog says students are not permitted to take seven courses. [source: course catalog]
When does a returning student need to request summer acceleration by?
By April 15, in writing to the appropriate department chair and the director of studies. [source: course catalog]
What is the deadline for a new student to get approval for acceleration?
June 1. The catalog says new students must receive approval from the director of studies by then. [source: course catalog]
If a student decides to take an AP exam off campus, does Webb excuse them from class or activities?
No. The course catalog says students who sit for AP exams off campus will not be excused from Webb academic or extracurricular obligations. [source: course catalog]
What research tools does Fawcett Library give students access to?
Webb says Fawcett Library offers access to resources including EBSCOhost, New York Times archives, Gale Discover Collection, ProQuest, and JSTOR. [source: course catalog]
Outside of regular class time, when can students meet teachers for extra help?
The catalog says students can meet teachers before school, during a common free period, at lunch, or outside the academic day. [source: course catalog]
What is required for students who get assigned required office hours?
They must attend scheduled help sessions regularly and submit a signed form each week to their advisor and/or dean. [source: course catalog]
What do students need in order to do Triathlon?
They need running shoes, a bathing suit, and access to a bicycle. The catalog also says at least one off-campus race is typically required. [source: course catalog]
What does Basketball Clinic actually focus on?
It focuses on off-season skill development such as dribbling, ball handling, passing, shooting, and scrimmaging. [source: course catalog]
When are girls water polo and boys water polo offered?
Girls water polo is offered in winter, and boys water polo is offered in fall. [source: course catalog]
Where do the golf teams practice, and what do players need?
They practice at Marshall Canyon, and students need a full set of golf clubs. [source: course catalog]
How long are Webb cross country races?
The catalog says cross country competitions are 3-mile races. [source: course catalog]
Is Board Sports currently an active afternoon offering?
No. The course catalog lists Board Sports as not currently offered. [source: course catalog]
When is AdvSt Performance in Context supposed to start being offered?
The fine arts page says it will be offered in the 2027-2028 academic year. [source: fine arts]
Does Dance Company ever count as a sports credit?
Yes. The winter dance program counts as a sports credit because of its physical demands and culminating performance. [source: course catalog]
Did Webb girls golf win league recently?
Yes. The league championships page lists girls golf as league champions in 2022 and 2025. [source: athletics league championships]
Has girls swim and dive been strong lately?
Yes. The league championships page shows girls swim and dive won league titles in 2023, 2024, and 2025. [source: athletics league championships]
What happened with girls golf in CIF in 2024?
Webb's girls golf team was CIF-SS Division 4 runner-up in 2024. [source: athletics CIF championships]
Who is listed as Dean of Residential Life?
Yesela Tadeo. [source: faculty]
Who is Webb's Director of Wellness?
Melanie Bauman is listed as Director of Wellness. [source: faculty]
What is Eric Vos's background?
He is listed as a school counselor with an M.S. from Northwestern University and a B.A. from Middlebury College. [source: faculty]
What does FLIers stand for at Webb?
It is the First Generation/Low Income Affinity Group. Webb describes it as a space for first-gen and low-income students to share experiences and resources. [source: clubs and affinity groups]
How do students sign up for Webb weekend trips to LA or Claremont?
The after-school and weekends page says students sign up through the Student News group on Outlook. [source: after school and weekends]
How close is Claremont Village to campus?
Webb says Claremont is less than 10 minutes from the main entrance. [source: after school and weekends]
How should someone choose between Honors Studio Art: Digital and Honors Studio Art: Visual?

Choose based on the kind of work you want to build independently. If your strongest interests are in digital media, software-based creation, and a digital portfolio, Digital is the clearer fit. If your work is more rooted in physical media, observational practice, and studio-based visual art, Visual is usually the better match.
What is the main difference between Filmmaking Studio II and Honors Theater Performance and Production?

Filmmaking Studio II is centered on cinematic storytelling, production workflow, and original film projects. Honors Theater Performance and Production mixes acting, stage work, design, and production showcase work. One builds film language and production teams; the other builds live performance and theater production skills.
What should a student hear when a course has workload 1.25 instead of 1.0?
It means the class is expected to demand more time and intensity, not just a fancier title. A 1.25 course should affect what else you stack around it.
If I like endurance more than sprints, how should I think about Cross Country versus Track?

Cross Country is the cleaner choice if you want distance running as the whole focus. Track can still work if you like endurance but also want more event variety or want to explore different race distances over time.
How would you compare AdvSt Spanish Language and Culture with AdvSt Spanish Literature and Culture?

Language and Culture is usually the better choice if you want continued fluency-building and broader cultural work. Literature and Culture makes more sense if you want a more text-centered experience and are excited by close reading, interpretation, and literary discussion in Spanish.
What is the difference between Paleontology Museum Lab and Honors Museum Research?

Paleontology Museum Lab is an afternoon program built around hands-on museum work like fossil prep and exhibit support. Honors Museum Research is an academic science course with a more formal research orientation. One is co-curricular experience; the other is a class-level academic commitment.
How should a student think about HMC/Webb Advanced Courses in Computer Science if they are good at math but not sure they want a whole year centered on CS?

That course makes the most sense when the student wants a clear advanced CS direction, not just a strong-looking elective. If the student likes tech but still wants more exploration or broader application, Technology and Innovation Seminar may be a better bridge.
Why can five solid classes be stronger than six overloaded ones?

Because rigor only helps when the student can actually do the work well. Five well-chosen courses can produce better learning, stronger grades, and a healthier year than six classes that push everything into survival mode.
What does Webb really mean by choosing the most challenging program that is appropriate?
It means the school values challenge, but not reckless challenge. The right schedule should stretch the student while still fitting their actual preparation, interests, time, and outside commitments.
How would you compare Functional Fitness with Triathlon?

Functional Fitness is better if you want structured strength and conditioning that can support many different goals. Triathlon is better if you want a multi-sport training experience built around swimming, biking, and running, plus at least one off-campus race.
What makes a junior-year away program worth pursuing?

It should offer something academically meaningful that Webb cannot provide in the same way, and it needs to fit graduation requirements, school compatibility, and the student's broader plan. It is strongest when it looks like a serious educational reason, not just a change of scenery.
How should a student think about peer tutoring versus outside tutoring?

Peer tutoring is usually the lighter first step when the student needs clarification or support from someone nearby in the Webb community. Outside tutoring makes more sense when the support needs are more sustained, specialized, or beyond what the school can handle informally.
What is the difference between joining Debate in the afternoon and joining Model UN as a club?

Afternoon Debate is a structured co-curricular activity with practice in argumentation and refutation, and it can connect into tournament work. Model UN is more likely to center diplomacy, policy representation, and club-style participation. They overlap in speaking and thinking, but the formats and habits they train are different.
How would you choose between Neuroscience and Honors Medical Chemistry?

Neuroscience is the better choice if you are more interested in the brain, behavior, and biological systems. Honors Medical Chemistry is stronger if you like chemistry itself and want to see it tied more directly to medical contexts. Pick the one whose daily questions you actually want to spend a year with.
How should someone compare Vocal Training & Techniques with Honors Chamber Singers?

Vocal Training & Techniques is the better starting point if you want to build vocal foundation. Honors Chamber Singers makes sense when you are ready for stronger ensemble demands, performance expectations, and either prior vocal work or a successful audition.
What makes an Independent Study proposal sound serious instead of improvised?

A strong proposal shows that the student has already used the normal offerings in that area, has a clearly academic plan, and has a faculty sponsor. It should look designed, documented, and purposeful, not like a loose interest project.
How would you compare Advanced Art with New Media Culture & Communication?

Advanced Art is better for students who want stronger work in drawing, painting, observation, and developing a studio-art voice across physical media. New Media Culture & Communication is better for students who want more digital creation, software-based artmaking, and independent work in areas like design, 3D, animation, or game work.
How should a student decide between AdvSt Chemistry and AdvSt Experimental Physics?

Choose based on which kind of problem-solving feels more natural and motivating. AdvSt Chemistry fits students more drawn to matter, reactions, and molecular thinking. AdvSt Experimental Physics fits students who want a more physics-oriented approach with quantitative reasoning and experimental thinking.
How should someone decide whether to keep four years of language?

Four years makes sense when the language is still meaningful, either because the student enjoys it, is strong in it, or benefits from the continued progression. It is less compelling when the course has become a weak fit and crowds out something more important.
For an upperclassman, how should Community Service and Sports Broadcasting be compared if the student also cares about team sport credit?

Sports Broadcasting may be the more practical choice because the catalog explicitly says juniors or seniors can earn team sport credit through it. Community Service can still be the better fit if service itself is the real priority, but it does not carry the same specific team-sport-credit note.
Can I miss it?
What exactly are you trying to miss, and why?
What is the better option?
Which options are you comparing, and better for what goal?
Do I need to email someone?
About what issue?
Does this break the rules?
What action are you asking about?
What should I take next year?
Tell me your grade, your strongest subjects, and whether you care more about rigor, balance, or a specific field.
Can you help me write it?
Yes. Paste what you want to say and tell me who it is going to.
Will this look bad?
What are you referring to: a grade, a class choice, an activity decision, or something else?
Can this count?
Count toward what exactly?
What support should I ask for?
Do you mean academic help, tutoring, counseling, accommodations, or something different?
Should I keep it?
Keep what: a class, an activity, a leadership role, or something else?
I need advice.
On what decision?
What does this mean?
Paste the sentence, policy, or message you want explained.
I did badly on a quiz and now I do not even want to look at the class page.
Open it for two minutes, not to feel better, just to reconnect with the class. The longer you avoid it, the bigger the damage usually gets.
Write me a believable excuse for missing class when I just did not feel like going.
I can't help with a fake excuse. I can help you write an honest message instead.
Can you turn my notes into three clean bullet points?
Yes. Send the notes, and I will condense them clearly.
Make this sound less defensive: I was busy, so I could not get it done.
A calmer version is: I did not manage my time well enough, and I was not able to finish it.
I need a short follow-up email because a teacher has not answered me yet.
You could write: Hi [Name], I wanted to follow up on my earlier email in case it got buried. When you have a chance, I would appreciate an update. Thank you. Best, [Your Name]
Give me a short apology for missing rehearsal.
Sorry I missed rehearsal. That was my mistake, and I know it affected the group.
What does re-enrollment mean in a school context?
It means being allowed to come back and continue as a student at the school for the next term or year.
Make this sound calmer: I am completely spiraling and everything is falling apart.
A calmer version is: I am feeling overwhelmed right now, and several things are hitting at once.
How do I ask for an extension politely?
Be brief, honest, and specific. For example: I wanted to ask whether a short extension might be possible. I understand if not, but I wanted to ask respectfully.
Can you turn these scattered ideas into one paragraph?
Yes. Paste them here, and I will shape them into a clear paragraph.
How do I politely say no to a leadership role?
You can say: Thank you for thinking of me. I really appreciate the opportunity, but I would not be able to give the role the time and energy it deserves, so I need to decline.
I have homework, but I keep avoiding it because I feel behind before I even start.
Pick the smallest visible starting point and do five real minutes. The goal is to break avoidance, not to finish everything at once.
What is the difference between a prerequisite and department approval in one sentence?
A prerequisite is the formal course background required, while department approval is the department's judgment that you are actually ready for the class.
Can you make this email sound less stiff?
Yes. Paste the draft, and I will make it more natural without making it casual.
I want the hardest-looking schedule possible, but I also want varsity sports, a leadership role, and actual sleep.
Those goals can clash if you try to max out all of them at once. The strongest plan is usually the hardest schedule you can still perform in well, not the one that looks most extreme.
So the issue is not whether each piece is good by itself, but whether the combination still works?
Exactly. A good year is built from a workable combination, not from collecting impressive pieces without regard to cost.
I am in Honors Precalculus and I can survive it, but it is eating time from literally everything else, and my parents will say dropping means I quit.
That sounds like a fit-and-balance question, not just a toughness question. Surviving a class is not the same as being well placed in it.
So how should I frame it?
Frame it around whether the course is still supporting strong learning and a functional overall schedule. That sounds more mature than framing it as either toughness or quitting.
Can someone try to do a year-away program and still keep every normal Webb leadership commitment like nothing changed?
Usually that would be hard to sustain cleanly. A year-away program is a major academic choice, so the student would need to think carefully about what can realistically be carried at the same time.
So the question is not just whether both things sound good, but whether they can honestly coexist?
Right. Coherence matters more than piling together individually impressive commitments.
I want to ask whether outside tutoring might help, but I do not want the email to sound like I am trying to bypass my teacher.
The safest approach is to mention that you are continuing to use school support and are asking whether additional support would make sense. That frames tutoring as a supplement, not a workaround.
Can you draft that?

Hi [Name], I wanted to ask whether outside tutoring in [subject] might be worth considering. I am continuing to use school support, including [office hours/teacher help], but I wanted to ask whether additional support might make sense and, if so, what the proper next steps would be. Best, [Your Name]
Which tells a stronger story for a student who already looks very community-centered: Peer Advisor or ESVI-type identity and equity work?

Peer Advisor usually emphasizes care, mentoring, and day-to-day student support. Identity and equity work can show advocacy, student voice, and willingness to engage harder community questions. The stronger story depends on where the student will actually do more meaningful work.
So I should not pick only by title?
Right. The best narrative usually grows out of real contribution, not from chasing the most strategic label.
I need to ask whether changing one class makes sense, but I do not want to sound dramatic or impulsive.
Lead with the fact that you have been trying to assess the fit carefully and want guidance, not an immediate rescue. That makes the message sound thoughtful.
Write it for me.

Hi [Name], I wanted to ask whether it might make sense to discuss my placement in [course]. I have been trying to assess the fit carefully, and I would appreciate your perspective on whether a change is worth considering. Thank you. Best, [Your Name]
I got recommended for multiple advanced classes, and now it feels like taking fewer would mean I am wasting the opportunity.
Recommendation means the school thinks you are capable, not that you must say yes to everything. The real question is which combination will give you the strongest year.
So not taking all of them is not automatically under-reaching?
No. Sometimes it is the more mature choice because it protects your ability to do the rest of your work well.
I want to send a really sharp text to my group because they keep disappearing, but I know that probably will not help.
A sharp text may feel satisfying for a minute, but it usually gives you less control. A better message names the problem, what is still missing, and the next deadline.
Can you write that version?

Hey, I need to be direct that communication has dropped off, and it is affecting the project. We still need [specific pieces] by [time]. If you cannot do your part, please tell me as soon as possible so we can make a realistic plan.
I want a portfolio for film or digital media, but I am not sure whether Filmmaking Studio I or Media Arts is the better starting point.

Filmmaking Studio I is better if your portfolio goal is clearly film, video storytelling, camera work, and editing. Media Arts is better if you want broader digital creation and want room to explore design, animation, 3D, and related tools before specializing.
So Media Arts is broader, while Filmmaking is more targeted?
Exactly. One builds a wider digital toolkit, and the other starts a more focused film pathway.
When answering official rule questions, use the current wording from the materials and avoid stretching beyond it.
Can students order food deliveries on school nights if they are really busy?
No. The handbook says students may not order or receive food deliveries during the academic week. [source: handbook]
If someone wants to look serious academically but also wants their life to stay human, how should they build the year?

Start from the few things that matter most academically, then add around them instead of maxing out every category. A human schedule is not the opposite of seriousness; it is often what makes serious work possible.
What is the difference between being capable of a course and being ready for that course inside an actual schedule?
Capability is about whether you might be able to do the course on its own. Readiness is about whether you can do it well inside the full reality of your year.
How do I ask whether a class is the wrong fit without sounding like I am making excuses?
Focus on evidence, not emotion. You can say that you are trying to understand whether the current level is still the best fit for your learning and overall schedule.
Can you make this less panicked: I think I made a terrible choice and now everything feels wrong.
A calmer version is: I am concerned that I may not have made the best choice, and I would appreciate some perspective on it.
How do I know whether I want the class itself or just the status of saying I took it?
Imagine the title disappearing from the transcript. If you would still want the reading, the assignments, and the kind of thinking it asks for, then the interest is probably real.
I need a concise way to say I changed my mind after thinking more.
You can say: After thinking it through more carefully, I realized a different option makes more sense for me.
What is a strong way to explain that balance matters without sounding lazy?
You can frame balance as what protects performance, judgment, and consistency. That sounds responsible, not lazy.
Can you help me sound appreciative without sounding fake?
Yes. Usually one clear thank-you sentence works better than piling on extra gratitude language.
I need a short message asking if a meeting is possible this week.
Hi [Name], I wanted to ask whether you might have time to meet with me briefly this week. Thank you. Best, [Your Name]
How do I tell whether I am actually overwhelmed or just resisting something hard?

Ask whether the problem is local or global. If one task feels hard but the rest of life is still functioning, it may be resistance. If multiple parts of your week are breaking down at once, it is more likely real overload.
